name: Julie Ilcin
description: A resident with deep roots in neighborhood association networks who brings organizing experience and knowledge of city government processes.
source_text: Julie Ilcin is a resident with deep roots in Euclid's neighborhood association network. She brings organizing experience, relationships with block club leaders across the city, and knowledge of city government processes.

name: Martha Nieset
description: A longtime resident and community connector with relationships across faith communities and civic organizations, experienced in community documentation and storytelling.
gifts: relationships across faith communities and schools
source_text: Martha Nieset is a longtime resident and community connector. She has relationships across faith communities, schools, and civic organizations, and brings experience in community documentation and storytelling.

name: Kay O'Donnell
description: Director of the Euclid Hunger Center who brings institutional resources, food access networks, and knowledge of which families need support across the city.
source_text: Kay O'Donnell is the Director of the Euclid Hunger Center, bringing institutional resources, a network of food access partners, and knowledge of which families are most in need across the city.

name: Stephen Langel
description: Works in mainstream public media and brings communications skills, media relationships, and the ability to amplify community stories to wider audiences.
source_text: Stephen Langel works in mainstream public media and brings communications skills, media relationships, and the ability to amplify community stories to wider audiences.

name: Corey Russell
description: Principal of Euclid High School who brings institutional authority, relationships with hundreds of families, access to school facilities, and knowledge of youth needs.
source_text: Corey Russell is the Principal of Euclid High School, bringing institutional authority, relationships with hundreds of families, access to school facilities, and knowledge of youth needs.

name: Demetrius Williams
description: Youth Ambassador who brings firsthand knowledge of youth needs, peer networks, and the credibility to engage young people.
source_text: Demetrius Williams is a Youth Ambassador bringing firsthand knowledge of what youth in Euclid need, peer networks, and the energy and credibility to engage young people.

name: Deb Kroemer
description: A resident with community history expertise who has documented neighborhood stories and transitions over three decades, serving as a living archive of cultural memory.
source_text: Deb Kroemer is a resident with community history expertise, having documented neighborhood stories and transitions in Euclid over three decades. She is a living archive of the city's cultural memory.

name: Euclid Democratic Club
description: A civic organizing hub that has operated for decades with strong turnout infrastructure and relationships across the city's neighborhoods.
source_text: The Euclid Democratic Club has been a civic organizing hub for decades, with strong turnout infrastructure and relationships across the city's neighborhoods.

name: Neighbor Circles
description: Structured spaces created through the Mayor's Office that allow residents to connect with city leadership.
source_text: Neighbor Circles through the Mayor's Office create structured spaces for residents to connect with city leadership.

name: Youth Council in Euclid
description: An organizational partner committed to helping youth identify issues and solve their own problems with resources and support.
source_text: Youth Council in Euclid, Resource Clearinghouse.

name: Euclid Hunger Center
description: A formal organization addressing food access and supporting families in need across the city.
source_text: Kay O'Donnell is the Director of the Euclid Hunger Center, bringing institutional resources, a network of food access partners, and knowledge of which families are most in need across the city.

name: Euclid High School
description: A formal educational institution serving hundreds of families and providing access to school facilities and resources.
source_text: Corey Russell is the Principal of Euclid High School, bringing institutional authority, relationships with hundreds of families, access to school facilities, and knowledge of youth needs.

name: Schools in Euclid
description: Trusted anchors in every neighborhood serving as community institutions with facilities available for community use.
source_text: Schools in Euclid serve as trusted anchors in every neighborhood and have gymnasiums, meeting rooms, and green spaces available for community use.

name: Euclid Collaborative
description: An organizational partner that comprises a website and booklet of all resources in Euclid, serving as a resource coordination hub.
source_text: Euclid Collaborative comprising a website and booklet of all resources in Euclid.

name: Resource Clearinghouse
description: An organizational partner serving as a resource hub where the police force refers people to access support.
source_text: Police force will refer people to resource center.

name: Euclid Police Force
description: A formal institution that partners with community resources by referring people to support services and resource centers.
source_text: Police force will refer people to resource center.

name: Sims Park
description: A beloved community gathering space where summer concerts and cultural events have been held for generations, rooted in 40 years of outdoor community celebration tradition.
source_text: Sims Park is a beloved gathering space where summer concerts and cultural events have been held for generations, rooted in a tradition of outdoor community celebration that dates back 40 years.

name: Euclid neighborhoods
description: Physical neighborhoods that serve as the foundation for localized community organizing and connection.
source_text: Street meetings, localized groups in neighborhoods.

name: Sims Park tradition
description: A 40-year tradition of outdoor community celebration through summer concerts and food that represents shared cultural identity and belonging.
source_text: Sims Park is a beloved gathering space where summer concerts and cultural events have been held for generations, rooted in a tradition of outdoor community celebration that dates back 40 years.

name: Euclid's neighborhood history and memory
description: Documented community stories and transitions that represent the shared cultural heritage and history of Euclid's neighborhoods.
source_text: Deb Kroemer is a resident with community history expertise, having documented neighborhood stories and transitions in Euclid over three decades. She is a living archive of the city's cultural memory.

name: Willis Brown
description: A resident and longtime small business owner with deep knowledge of the local economy and mentoring experience with young entrepreneurs.
source_text: Willis Brown is a resident and longtime small business owner on Euclid Avenue. He has deep knowledge of the local economy, relationships with the Chamber of Commerce, and has mentored several young entrepreneurs in the city.

name: Andre Brice
description: A resident who runs an informal job readiness program connecting unemployed residents with local employers.
source_text: Andre Brice runs an informal job readiness program out of his home, connecting unemployed residents with local employers. He has placed over 30 people in jobs in the last two years.

name: Cheryl Mays
description: A resident and business owner who operates a catering business and regularly donates meals to community events.
source_text: Cheryl Mays is both a resident and business owner. She operates a catering business that sources food from local suppliers and regularly donates meals to community events.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity - Euclid Chapter
description: An active fraternal organization with community service programming including scholarship funds and youth mentorship.
source_text: Kappa Alpha Psi fraternity has a chapter in Euclid with active community service programming including scholarship funds and youth mentorship.

name: Kiwanis Club of Euclid
description: A long-established service organization with 30 years of organizing community projects and strong institutional connections.
source_text: Kiwanis Club of Euclid has been organizing community service projects and fundraisers for 30 years, with strong institutional connections to local schools and government.

name: Juneteenth Block Party Coalition
description: A coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents that has organized an annual celebration for 12 years.
source_text: Euclid's annual Juneteenth block party, organized by a coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents, is a 12-year tradition that celebrates African American heritage and spotlights local entrepreneurs.

name: Pope's Hardware
description: A family-owned business that donates tools and materials to neighborhood improvement projects.
source_text: Pope's Hardware on Euclid Avenue has been family-owned for three generations and donates tools and materials to neighborhood improvement projects.

name: Cheryl Mays' Catering Business
description: A local catering business that sources from local suppliers and contributes food to community events.
source_text: She operates a catering business that sources food from local suppliers and regularly donates meals to community events.

name: Willis Brown's Small Business
description: A longtime small business on Euclid Avenue that provides local employment and economic knowledge.
source_text: Willis Brown is a resident and longtime small business owner on Euclid Avenue. He has deep knowledge of the local economy, relationships with the Chamber of Commerce, and has mentored several young entrepreneurs in the city.

name: Pope's Hardware - Family Business
description: A three-generation family-owned business providing local employment and economic activity.
source_text: Pope's Hardware on Euclid Avenue has been family-owned for three generations and donates tools and materials to neighborhood improvement projects.

name: Juneteenth Celebration
description: A 12-year tradition celebrating African American heritage and community identity.
source_text: Euclid's annual Juneteenth block party, organized by a coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents, is a 12-year tradition that celebrates African American heritage and spotlights local entrepreneurs.

name: Black-Owned Business Community
description: Local entrepreneurs and business owners who maintain cultural heritage and community identity through their enterprises.
source_text: Euclid's annual Juneteenth block party, organized by a coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents, is a 12-year tradition that celebrates African American heritage and spotlights local entrepreneurs.

name: Karla
description: Long-term Euclid Public Library employee with 29 years of institutional knowledge, community relationships, and library programming expertise.
source_text: Karla has worked at the Euclid Public Library for 29 years and has deep institutional knowledge of community resources and a trusted presence with longtime residents. She brings reference expertise, community relationships, and library programming experience.

name: Mary Gay
description: Licensed social worker with expertise in trauma-informed care, community facilitation, and connections to the Euclid development community.
source_text: Mary Gay is a licensed social worker with expertise in trauma-informed care, community facilitation, and connection to the Euclid development community.

name: Michael
description: Committed community member advocate working to address trauma and violence in Euclid.
source_text: Michael is committed as a community member advocate.

name: Rachel
description: Euclid resident with 4 years of community experience and participation in trauma-focused visioning.
source_text: Rachel (Euclid resident 4 years)

name: Jaylen
description: Youth participant age 14 involved in community visioning and connected to CCLE.
source_text: Jaylen (youth, 14, CCLE)

name: Bev
description: Ally resident with community preference literacy (CPL) contributing to Euclid Hope Ambassador Program capacity discussions.
source_text: Bev (ally resident, CPL)

name: Euclid Public Library
description: Formal municipal institution serving as a community resource hub and trusted gathering place with 29 years of continuous programming and community relationships.
source_text: Karla has worked at the Euclid Public Library for 29 years and has deep institutional knowledge of community resources and a trusted presence with longtime residents.

name: Cleveland Peacemakers Alliance
description: Multi-city organization providing conflict resolution training and violence interruption programming with demonstrated track record across Northeast Ohio.
source_text: Cleveland Peacemakers Alliance brings conflict resolution training and violence interruption programming with a track record across multiple Northeast Ohio cities.

name: Life Anew Behavioral Health
description: Mental health service provider offering trauma-informed counseling and recovery services to community members.
source_text: Life Anew Behavioral Health provides trauma-informed counseling and recovery services.

name: Euclid Hospital Emergency Department
description: First point of contact for residents in crisis with pilot community health worker follow-up program.
source_text: The Euclid Hospital Emergency Department serves as a first point of contact for many residents experiencing crisis and has begun piloting community health worker follow-up.

name: Moore Counseling and Mediation
description: Mental health service provider offering culturally responsive mental health services to the community.
source_text: Moore Counseling and Mediation offers culturally responsive mental health services.

name: Village of Healing
description: Community-based organization providing peer support services for trauma survivors.
source_text: Village of Healing is a community-based organization providing peer support for trauma survivors.

name: BEST Community Resource Center
description: Resource center connecting residents to wraparound services and comprehensive support systems.
source_text: BEST Community Resource Center connects residents to wraparound services.

name: Neighbor Night
description: Community gathering and connection initiative that brings neighbors together.
source_text: Resources and assets named: beach clubs, Neighbor Night.

name: Beach clubs
description: Informal community groups and gathering spaces that foster resident connections and social cohesion.
source_text: Resources and assets named: beach clubs, Neighbor Night.

name: Euclid Healing Walk
description: Annual community-organized gathering that brings residents together for collective grieving and hope-building around violence prevention.
source_text: Euclid's annual Healing Walk, a tradition started 8 years ago by community members who lost loved ones to violence, draws hundreds of residents each spring in a march through the city's neighborhoods as an act of communal grief and hope.

name: Euclid Healing Walk
description: Eight-year community tradition honoring those lost to violence through annual spring march, transmitting shared grief, resilience, and collective identity.
source_text: Euclid's annual Healing Walk, a tradition started 8 years ago by community members who lost loved ones to violence, draws hundreds of residents each spring in a march through the city's neighborhoods as an act of communal grief and hope.

name: Kathleen McDonnel
description: A longtime youth advocate with deep relationships across school and rec center networks who brings facilitation skills, community trust, and connections to city leadership.
source_text: Kathleen McDonnel is a longtime youth advocate in Euclid with deep relationships across school and rec center networks. She brings facilitation skills, community trust, and connections to city leadership.

name: Sierra Stallworth
description: A young resident and emerging community leader who brings lived experience as Euclid youth and has a network of peers.
source_text: Sierra Stallworth is a young resident and emerging community leader who brings lived experience as Euclid youth, note-taking skills, and a network of peers.

name: Damian Calvert
description: A community member who has been organizing youth sports leagues informally and has deep knowledge of vulnerable youth and their families.
source_text: Damian Calvert has been organizing youth sports leagues informally in the neighborhood for three years. He knows which kids are falling through the cracks and has relationships with their families.

name: Euclid Boys League
description: A formal organization that has been running youth athletics in the city for over 20 years, providing structured sports programming and a consistent gathering point.
source_text: The Euclid Boys League has been running youth athletics in the city for over 20 years and serves as a weekly gathering point for boys ages 8-16.

name: Elevated Dance Headquarters
description: A formal organization providing after-school dance programming rooted in hip hop and African dance traditions.
source_text: Elevated Dance Headquarters offers after-school dance programming rooted in hip hop and African dance traditions that have been part of Euclid's cultural fabric for over a decade.

name: Elevated Dance Headquarters
description: This organization transmits hip hop and African dance traditions that have been part of Euclid's cultural fabric for over a decade.
source_text: Elevated Dance Headquarters offers after-school dance programming rooted in hip hop and African dance traditions that have been part of Euclid's cultural fabric for over a decade.

name: The Upstage Players
description: A formal theater group with a 15-year tradition of producing community theater with youth cast members.
source_text: The Upstage Players theater group has a 15-year tradition of producing community theater with youth cast members, drawing on Euclid's rich history of neighborhood storytelling.

name: The Upstage Players
description: This theater group draws on Euclid's rich history of neighborhood storytelling, transmitting local narratives and heritage through performance.
source_text: The Upstage Players theater group has a 15-year tradition of producing community theater with youth cast members, drawing on Euclid's rich history of neighborhood storytelling.

name: Front porch gatherings
description: An informal tradition where neighborhood elders gather to share community knowledge and stories, representing collective community capacity and intergenerational connection.
source_text: Parris noted that there is an informal tradition in her neighborhood of elders hosting front porch gatherings every summer where older residents share stories about what Euclid used to be like, passing down neighborhood history to younger generations.

name: Front porch gatherings
description: An informal tradition of elders sharing stories about neighborhood history, transmitting local knowledge and cultural memory across generations.
source_text: Parris noted that there is an informal tradition in her neighborhood of elders hosting front porch gatherings every summer where older residents share stories about what Euclid used to be like, passing down neighborhood history to younger generations.

name: Euclid HOPE Task Force - Youth Safety Group
description: An informal community group formed to address youth safety through visioning and collective action, representing community coordination and shared commitment.
source_text: Euclid HOPE Task Force - Visioning Session, May 17 2025 Group: Youth Safety
